body.u: As I now look back to the days of when I first left school I think about my mates who I used to work in the shirt factory on the isle of Sheppey with and what times we use to have going out with our boyfriends to disco and youth clubs just like youngsters do nowadays. It was a great experience for me to go and collect my wage packet, my first ever wage packet from the bossesboss's office, I use to make up wonderful dreams on how I would go off to Canada and marry a rich millionaire and we would live happy ever after, just like it was said in story books but instead I met Robert. Robert was a year younger than myself but we used to have fun going out together, motorbike racing, to discos, swimming, and his age didn’t seem to matter to me at all. I can always remember how he proposed to me, he fell down a flight of stairs and landed in my arms and said “Darling will you marry me”, and of course that was it.

We had moved from Sheppey up into Ascon just outside Doncaster and that where our four little girls grew up and got married. Robert was a motorbike racer up theirthere and I got a job as a bar assistant. It was quite pleasant really because you met different kinds of people.

Although I didn’t mind Robert going racing, I was always sure some kind of accident would happen and it did, his bike crashed and he was taken to hospital with a broken back, legs, his pelvis and he lost the sight in his left eye. When he came out from hospital he was confined to a wheelchair, so I had to leave my job as a bar assistant and stay at home with Robert to look after him.
That’s now it is {rocky?} my Robert in a chair and my four daughters grown up with children of their own, but to me it was all I ever really wanted.
